skill.name: cloud-devops
skill.description: Cloud infrastructure and DevOps workflow covering AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, monitoring, and cloud-native development.
skill.summary: Comprehensive cloud and DevOps workflow bundle for infrastructure provisioning, container orchestration, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation bundle containing markdown workflow guides, not executable code. Static analyzer flagged 66 instances of 'backtick execution' which are actually Markdown code fence delimiters used for formatting prompts. The 'C2 keywords' and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives from matching AWS service names (EC2, ECS, EKS) and documentation text. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Deploy and manage cloud infrastructure with DevOps best practices
content.value_statement: Managing cloud infrastructure across multiple providers requires coordinated workflows. This skill bundle provides structured phases for infrastructure provisioning, container orchestration, CI/CD, monitoring, security, and cost optimization.
actual_capabilities: Design multi-cloud architecture across AWS, Azure, and GCP
Provision infrastructure using Terraform and cloud-native tools
Orchestrate containers with Kubernetes and Docker
Implement CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Configure monitoring with Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog
Optimize cloud costs and implement disaster recovery
limitations: This skill provides guidance and prompts, not direct cloud resource deployment
Requires valid cloud credentials and permissions for actual infrastructure changes
Depends on availability of referenced sub-skills for specialized tasks
Assumes user has baseline DevOps and cloud platform knowledge

use_cases.title: Multi-cloud infrastructure setup
use_cases.description: Design and provision infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP using Terraform with proper networking, IAM, and security configurations.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes deployment pipeline
use_cases.description: Containerize applications, create Helm charts, and deploy to Kubernetes clusters with automated CI/CD workflows.

use_cases.title: Observability stack implementation
use_cases.description: Set up comprehensive monitoring with metrics collection, log aggregation, distributed tracing, and alerting dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Cloud Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new cloud environment on AWS. I need VPC networking, EC2 instances, RDS database, and proper IAM roles. Start with a basic architecture and explain each component.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up first cloud infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Container Orchestration Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Node.js application that needs to run on Kubernetes. Help me create the Dockerfile, K8s manifests, and Helm chart for deployment with proper resource limits and health checks.

prompt_templates.title: CI/CD Pipeline Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for my Python project that runs tests, builds a Docker image, pushes to ECR, and deploys to EKS on merge to main. Include rollback capability.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Phase Infrastructure Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through a complete cloud migration: Phase 1 - Design architecture for multi-tier app on AWS. Phase 2 - Provision with Terraform. Phase 3 - Containerize and deploy to EKS. Phase 4 - Set up CloudWatch and Prometheus monitoring. Phase 5 - Implement cost optimization. Provide commands and configurations for each phase.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team lead managing full infrastructure project

output_examples.input: Design a highly available web application architecture on AWS
output: - VPC with public and private subnets across 3 AZs
- Application Load Balancer in public subnets
- Auto Scaling Group of EC2 instances in private subnets
- ElastiCache Redis cluster for session caching
- S3 bucket for static assets with CloudFront distribution
- NAT Gateways for outbound internet access
- Security Groups with least-privilege access rules

output_examples.input: Create a Terraform configuration for EKS cluster
output: - EKS control plane with managed node groups
- IAM OIDC provider for service accounts
- CloudWatch logging enabled for control plane
- Private subnet placement for worker nodes

best_practices: Use infrastructure as code (Terraform) for all cloud resources to ensure reproducibility and version control
Implement the principle of least privilege for IAM roles and service accounts
Enable comprehensive logging and monitoring before deploying to production
anti_patterns: Deploying resources without disaster recovery and backup strategies
Hardcoding credentials instead of using secrets management solutions
Skipping cost monitoring and alerts until receiving unexpectedly large bills

faq.question: What cloud providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: This workflow supports AWS, Azure, and GCP. Each provider has dedicated sections with relevant services and skills for architecture, deployment, and security.

faq.question: Do I need existing cloud accounts to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need active accounts with your chosen cloud provider(s) and appropriate permissions to provision resources. The skill provides guidance but cannot create accounts or bypass authentication.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use in production environments?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill follows cloud security best practices including IAM configuration, network segmentation, and encryption. However, always review generated configurations and test in non-production first.

faq.question: Can this skill help with cloud cost optimization?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 6 covers cost optimization including resource right-sizing, auto-scaling configuration, reserved instance recommendations, and setting up cost alerts and budgets.

faq.question: What Kubernetes distributions are supported?
faq.answer: The skill covers EKS (AWS), AKS (Azure), GKE (GCP), and generic Kubernetes configurations. Helm charts and manifests are compatible with any conformant Kubernetes cluster.

faq.question: How does this skill handle secrets and credentials?
faq.answer: The skill recommends using cloud-native secrets management (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager) and Kubernetes Secrets with encryption. Never store credentials in code or configuration files.
